title: The One With Two Parts, part 1

lines.speaker: With Minot Adjustments by
lines.text: Tennant Stuart
Rift's Restaurant, as seen in Mad About You, Joey and Chandler are there.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: This is unbelievable. It's been like a half an hour. If this was a cartoon, you'd be looking like a ham right about now.
Ursula Buffay, Phoebe's identical twin sister, is waiting on tables in her inimitable manner.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: There's the waitress. Excuse me, Miss. Hello, Miss?
Ursula spins around looking puzzled, quite unable to tell where the sound is coming from.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's Phoebe! Hi!
Ursula notices Joey waving his hand, and comes over.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Hi. Okay, will that be all?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wait, wait! Wh-what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah, um, I was over there, then you said, "Excuse me, hello Miss," so now I'm here.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: No, no... how come you are working here?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Right, yeah, 'cause its close to where I live, and the aprons are really cute.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Can we start over?

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah. Okay great. I'm gonna be over here. (She wanders away.)

lines.speaker: Chandler and Joey
lines.text: No, no, no!
Opening Credits
A wintry February day in New York City, snowplows are clearing the streets. Inside Central Perk, all three girls are paying court to Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I don't know whether he's testing me, or just acting out, but my monkey is out of control. But, he keeps erasing the messages on my machine, "supposedly" by accident.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, yeah, I've done that.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: And then, like three days in a row he got to the newspaper before I did, and peed all over the crossword.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I've never done that.
Outside in the street, Joey and Chandler arrive, to peer through the window at Phoebe, by bending down to look underneath the shop's sign-a large steaming cup of coffee.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: All right, now look at her and tell me she doesn't look exactly like her sister.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I'm sayin' I see a difference.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: They're twins!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't care. Phoebe's Phoebe. Ursula's... hot!
Joey and Chandler come indoors.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know that thing, when you and I talk to each other about things?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Let's not do that any more.
They hang up their coats and scarves, then approach their friends on the main sofa.

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: Hey guys! Hey!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey Pheebs, guess who we saw today.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, ooh, fun! Okay... um, Liam Neeson.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Morly Safer.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Nope.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: The woman who cuts my hair!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, look, this could be a really long game.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Your sister Ursula.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Her face dropping) Oh, really.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, yeah, she works over at that place, uh...

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Rift's. Yeah, I know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, you do? Because she said you guys haven't talked in like years.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hmmm? Yeah. So, um, is she fat?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Not from where I was standin'.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Turning to Chandler) where were you standing?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Um, Pheebs, so, you guys just don't get along?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: It's mostly just dumb sister stuff, you know, I mean, like, everyone always thought of her as the pretty one, you know... Oh, oh, she was the first one to start walking, even though I did it... later that same day. But, to my parents, by then it was like "yeah, right, well what else is new?"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, Pheebs, I'm sorry, I've got to go. I've got Lamaze class.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, and I've got Earth Science, but I'll catch you in Gym.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, is this just gonna be you and Carol?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, Susan's gonna be there too. We've got dads, we've got lesbians, the whole parenting team.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, isn't, isn't that gonna be weird?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, no. (Distractedly putting on a jacket to go out) I mean, it mighta been at first, but by now I, I think I'm pretty comfortable with the whole situation.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, that's my jacket.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I know.
Rachel grins as Ross removes the girlie jacket, grabs his own, and rushes out.
The Lamaze class, several couples and one trio sit on the floor, introducing themselves to the teacher, who's got as far as a woman sitting next to Ross, Carol, and Susan.

lines.speaker: Woman
lines.text: Hi, we're the Rostins. Err, I'm J.C., and he's Michael, and we're having a boy, and a girl.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Good for you. Alrighty, next?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi, um, I'm err, (has to clear his throat) I'm Ross Geller, and err ah... (pats Carol's bulge) ..that's, that's my boy in there, and uh, (points) this is Carol Willick, and this... is Susan Bunch. Susan is um Carol's, just, com... (embarrassment finally overwhelms the poor fellow, who becomes incoherent until) ..who's next?

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: I'm sorry, I didn't get... Susan is?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Susan is Carol's, Carol's, Carol's, friend...

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Life partner.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Like buddies.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Like lovers.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You know how close women can get.
The teacher smiles, but her eyebrows go up. Susan and Carol pat each other affectionately.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: Susan and I live together.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Although I was married to her.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Carol, not me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Err, right.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: It's a little complicated.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: A little.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: But we're fine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Absolutely. (Turns back to the woman next to him.) So, twins... hah! That's like two births. (He struggles again.) Ouch.
Chandler's Office, Chandler is working.
Helen's buzzer is heard on the intercom, so Chandler presses his button, too.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And (he imitates the buzzer) to you too, Helen.

lines.speaker: Helen
lines.text: (Over the intercom) Nina Bookbinder is here to see you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, okay. Send her in.
He hurriedly checks his hair in his computer screen, before taking a sporting trophy from a drawer to place ostentatiously on his desk. An attractive young woman opens the door.

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hi, Nina. Come on in.

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: You wanted to see me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Uh, Yes. Yes. I've just been going over your data here, and little thing, you've been post-dating your Friday numbers.

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: Which is bad, because?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, it throws my WENUS out of whack.

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: Your... excuse me?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: WENUS. (Coughs) Weekly Estimated Net...

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: Oh, Net Usage Statistics, right. Gotcha, gotcha. Won't happen again. I wouldn't want to do anything to hurt your... "wenus."
Nina beams flirtatiously at Chandler, who catches her drift, but for once he's lost for something to say – so she nods her head to tell him that he's thinking correctly...
Monica and Rachel's, Ross, Chandler, and the girls are dividing some Chinese takeout, while the sitcom Family Matters is playing on the TV.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: It's not just that she's cute, okay. It's just that... she's really really cute.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It doesn't matter. You don't dip your pen in the company ink.
Marcel scampers about, interfering with the neatness.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ross, your little creature's got the remote again.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Marcel, Marcel, give Rossie the remote. Marcel. Marcel, you give Rossie the remote right now... Marce... you give Rossie the remote...
Marcel points the remote at Monica's television, pressing a particular combination of keys. The logo SAP appears on the screen, and suddenly the dialogue is dubbed into Spanish.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Great.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Relax, I'll fix it.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (Looking at the television) Cool... "Urkel" in Spanish is "Urkel."

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (looking at the remote) How did he do this?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Looking out at the balcony) So tell me something, is leaving the Christmas lights up part of your plan to keep us merry all year long?
Rachel slowly spins around, finally noticing that the lights have outstayed their welcome.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Ah no, you see, someone was supposed to take them down around New Year's... but obviously someone forgot.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, someone was supposed to write "Rach, take down the lights" and put it on the re... frigerate... (finally noticing Monica's note stuck to the refrigerator) How long has that been there?
Joey enters, looking extremely pleased with himself.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, where you been?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I went back to Riff's. I think Ursula likes me. All I ordered was coffee, she brought me a tuna meltt and four plates of curly fries.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Score.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: She is so hot!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah, listen. Okay, before you do anything Joey-like, you might wanna run it by err... (he indicates Phoebe, who is helping Ross understand the remote control.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Pheebs?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Jumping up) Yeah?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You think it would be okay if I asked out your sister?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Why? Why would you wanna... do that?  Why?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: So that if we went out on a date, she'd be there.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, I mean, I'm not my sister's, you know, whatever, and um... I mean, it's true, we were one egg, once, but err, you know, we've grown apart, so, um... I don't know, why not? Okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Cool, thanks.
He happily gestures at Chandler that there was nothing to worry about, then exits. Rachel and Monica are concerned for poor Phoebe, who slides back down next to Ross.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You okay?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah I'm fine.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: You wanna watch Laverne y Shirley?
The sitcom begins with its familiar refrain, yet with a Latin lilt. Rachel and Monica do a little dance with their chopsticks, and Phoebe has to grin as Ross joins in the rhythm.

Lamaze class. Susan is there. Each couple has a doll, for they have just finished learning how to change a diaper. As Ross rushes in, stepping on the Rostins' pretend baby, squashing its head flat. It bleats, in protest. He performs emergency surgery, then hands the doll back to J.C.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Sorry.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hi. Sorry I'm late. Where's, where's Carol?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Stuck at school. Some parent-teacher thing. You can go. I'll get the information.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No... No... No. I think I should stay, I think we should both know what's going on.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Oh, good. This'll be fun.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Alrighty. We're gonna start with some basic third stage breathing exercises, so Mummies, why don't you get on your back? And... coaches, you should be supporting Mummy's head.
Ross and Susan each gesture for the other to lie down.

lines.speaker: Ross and Susan
lines.text: What? What? What?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: I am supposed to be the mommy?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, I'm gonna play my sperm card one more time.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Look, I don't see why I should have to miss out on the coaching training just because I'm a woman.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I see. So what do you propose to do?

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: I will flip you for it.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Flip me for it? No, no, no... heads, heads, heads!

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: (Triumphantly) On your back... Mom.
Ross gets down like all the other mothers, cradled in Susan's lap like all the other fathers.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Alright, Mommies, take a nice deep cleansing breath.
Forgetting herself, Susan does the "Mommy" action with Ross.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Good. Now imagine your vagina is opening like a flower.
Ross comes out of character to glare into the distance.
Chandler's Office. Chandler is playing with a toy as his boss Mr. Douglas knocks and opens the door.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Mr. D, how's it going, sir?

lines.speaker: Mr. Douglas
lines.text: Ohh, it's been better. The Annual Net Usage Statistics are in.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And?

lines.speaker: Mr. Douglas
lines.text: It's pretty ugly. We haven't seen an ANUS this bad since the seventies.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: So what does this mean?

lines.speaker: Mr. Douglas
lines.text: Well, we're gonna be layin' off people in every department.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey, listen, I know I came in late last week, but I slept funny, and my hair was very very –

lines.speaker: Mr. Douglas
lines.text: Not you. Relax. Ever have to fire anyone?
Chandler's Office, later that day, Nina is in his office.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Nina? Nina. (He goes around his desk to where she is sitting.) Nina. (In pain) Nina.
She sympathetically reaches out to fondle the inner thigh of his left leg.

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: Are you okay?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Looking down at her hand) Yes, yes I am. Err, listen, the reason that I called you in here today was, err... please don't hate me.

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: (Taking her hand away) What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (Suddenly bright) Would you like to have dinner sometime?
Nina gasps in surprise and relief.
Central Perk, Rachel is at the counter, serving coffee to Phoebe.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So Pheebs, what do you want for your birthday?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, what I really want is for my mom to be alive and enjoy it with me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay... Let me put it this way. Anything from Crabtree and Evelyn?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh! Bath salts would be nice.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ooh, okay... good.
Jamie Buchman and Fran Devanow enter the coffee house. They look about them as Jamie removes her coat and scarf.

lines.speaker: Jamie
lines.text: What is this place?

lines.speaker: Fran
lines.text: Look, you're cold, I have to pee, and... (indicating the sign) ..there's a cup of coffee on the window. How bad could it be?
Jamie notices Phoebe sitting at the counter.

lines.speaker: Jamie
lines.text: I think we have an answer.

lines.speaker: Fran
lines.text: What's she doing here?

lines.speaker: Jamie
lines.text: This could be God's way of telling us to eat at home.

lines.speaker: Fran
lines.text: Think she got fired at Riff's?

lines.speaker: Jamie
lines.text: No, no, no. We were there last night. She kept... (shuddering at the memory) ..bringing swordfish. (Indicating the ladies' bathroom) are you gonna go to the, um?

lines.speaker: Fran
lines.text: I'm gonna wait till after we order. It's her, right.

lines.speaker: Jamie
lines.text: It looks like her.
Phoebe walks by, ignoring the two strangers.

lines.speaker: Jamie
lines.text: Um, excuse me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Jamie
lines.text: Hi, it's us.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Smiling blankly) Right, and it's me.

lines.speaker: Jamie
lines.text: So, so you're here too?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Much as you are.

lines.speaker: Jamie
lines.text: (Without moving her lips) Your turn.

lines.speaker: Fran
lines.text: Err... we know what we want.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: (Philosophically) Oh, that's good.

lines.speaker: Jamie
lines.text: All we want is two Caffe Lattes.

lines.speaker: Fran
lines.text: And some biscottie cookies.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Good choice.
Phoebe turns away so that the two weird women won't see the face she pulls, and sits down.

lines.speaker: Jamie
lines.text: Definitely her.

lines.speaker: Fran
lines.text: Yeah.
Commercial break

Monica and Rachel's, Phoebe is watching a Spanish version of The Waltons. At a nearby table sit Monica knitting, Rachel winding a ball of wool, and Chandler supplying them both from a skein which is spread between his hands.
Phoebe uses the remote to stop the Spanish by turning off the television.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can't believe you. You still haven't told that girl she doesn't have a job yet?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, you still haven't taken down the Christmas lights.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Congratulations, I think you've found the world's thinnest argument.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm just trying to find the right moment, you know?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, well, that shouldn't be so hard, now that you're dating. (Imitating men at their worst) "Sweetheart, you're fired, but how 'bout a quickie before I go to work?"
Joey lets himself in, carrying a large paper shopping bag.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel and Chandler
lines.text: Hey.
There is a loud knocking at the door through which Joey has just entered.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, once you're inside, you don't have to knock any more.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'll get it.
She rises, dragging Chandler along by the wool. Rachel has to leap over a chair to follow them. Monica opens the door to find Mr. Heckles standing there.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh. Hi, Mr.Heckles.

lines.speaker: Mr.Heckles
lines.text: You're doing it again.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We're not doing anything. We're just sitting around talking, quietly.

lines.speaker: Mr.Heckles
lines.text: I can hear you through the ceiling. My cats can't sleep.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You don't even have cats.

lines.speaker: Mr.Heckles
lines.text: I could have cats.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Closing the door) Goodbye Mr.Heckles.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: We'll try to keep it down.
The wool-bound trio returns to the table. Rachel has to rush ahead to avoid becoming tangled. Joey brings the shopping bag over to Phoebe, and takes out a nice cardigan.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Phoebe, could you do me a favour? Could you try this on? I just wanna make sure it fits.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ooh, my first birthday present... (delightedly examining the cardigan in her lap) ..oh, this is really...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Oh, no no no. It's for Ursula. I just figured, you know, size-wise.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Ohhh... Sure, yeah... (disgustedly dropping the cardigan back into the bag) ..okay, it fits.
The others have been taking all this in.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Are you seein' her again tonight?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yep. Ice Capades.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Wow, this is serious. I've never known you to pay money for any kind of capade.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I don't know. I like her, you know. She's different. There's uh, somethin' about her.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: That you like, (snappily confronting Joey over the heads of the knitting circle) we get it. You like her. Great!
The circle freezes in apprehension.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey, Phoebe, I asked you, and you said it was okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alright, well, maybe now it's not okay.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay... Well maybe now I'm not okay with it not being okay.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.
An embarrassed silence... finally broken by

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Knit, good woman, knit, knit!
Monica frantically bursts into action as Rachel resumes winding, tangling Chandler's wool.
Chandler's Office, Chandler & Nina are locked in a passionate embrace. Someone knocks, so they hurriedly separate to stare out of the window. Chandler's boss opens the door.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And that's the Chrysler Building right there.

lines.speaker: Mr. Douglas
lines.text: Nina.

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: Mr.Douglas... (flirting defensively) ..cool tie.
She escapes, fortunately so distracting Mr. Douglas, that he misses Chandler's expression of alarm & guilt.

lines.speaker: Mr. Douglas
lines.text: (Shutting the door, then pointing vaguely at Nina's shapely departure) She's still here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yes, yes she is. Didn't I memo you on this? See, after I let her go, err, I got a call from her psychiatrist, Dr. Flanen-nen, Dr. Flanen, Dr. Flan.
Thinking quickly, Chandler desperately tries to remember anything to do with schizophrenia....

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And err, he informed me that uh, she took the news rather badly, in fact, he uh, mentioned the word frenzy.

lines.speaker: Mr. Douglas
lines.text: You're kidding? She seems so...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, no, no. Nina... (miming fairies twinkling around his head) ..she is whooo wewee-woo whoo whoo! In fact, if you asked her right now, she would have no recollection of being fired at all, none at all.

lines.speaker: Mr. Douglas
lines.text: That's unbelievable.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And yet, believable. So I decided not to fire her again until I can be assured that she will be no threat to herself, or others.

lines.speaker: Mr. Douglas
lines.text: I see. I guess you never really know what's goin' on inside a person's head.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I guess that's why they call it psychology, sir.

Mr. Douglas screws up his eyes, trying to credit what Bing has just said, but turning to follow Nina down the corridor, he realises Bing must be telling the truth, since he would not have any personal interest in the girl, would he?

Lamaze class, Ross is again on the floor, cradled in Susan's lap, but now Carol is cradled in his lap, and she has a pretend baby, on her lap. The teacher is showing her class a video, which is about to end.

lines.speaker: Soothing male voice
lines.text: ..a sound Mom and Dad never forget. For this after all, is the miracle of birth.

lines.speaker: Teacher
lines.text: Lights please? And that's having a baby. Next week is our final class.
People start getting up. Ross grabs Carol's doll to hold it upside down like a football, slapping it with his other hand.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Susan, go deep.
Susan just glares back, as Ross's inappropriate joke falls flat. Meanwhile, a bubble is about to burst...

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: This is impossible. It's just impossible.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: What is, honey?

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: What that woman... did. I am not doin' that. It's just gonna have to stay in, that's all, everything will be the same, it'll just stay in.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Carol, honey, shhh, shhh, everything's gonna be alright.

lines.speaker: Carol
lines.text: (screaming at Ross) Oh, what do you know?  No one's going up to you and saying, "Hi, is that your nostril?  Mind if we push this pot roast through it?"

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: Carol, Carol, sweetie. Cleansing breath.
Both women gulp in air. Ross looks at his "football," then manipulates the head & limbs back into place, until it resembles what it represents.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: I know it's frightening, but, big picture. The birth part is just one day, and when it's over, we're all gonna be parents for the rest of our lives.
Ross is staring blankly into space.

lines.speaker: Susan
lines.text: I mean, that's what this is all about, right? Ross? Ross?
13: Central Perk, the gang is gathered around Monica comforting her brother, who in a slight state of shock is cuddling a cushion for security.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I'm gonna be a father.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: This is just occurring to you?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I always knew I was havin' a baby, I just never realised the baby was having me.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (She comforts him too) Oh, you're gonna be great!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Aw, how can you say that? I can't even get Marcel to stop eating the bath mat. How am I gonna raise a kid?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You know, Ross, some scientists are now saying that, that monkeys and babies are actually different.
Joey tires of this, so he gets up to leave.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Where're you going?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Out.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: With?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (Spreading his arms wide) Yes.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alright, could I just ask you one question?
Joey nods his head.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Have you two, you know... like... you know... you know... yet?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well, not that it's any of your business, but, no, we haven't, okay?
Joey walks toward the door, then hesitates and turns back.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You meant sex, right?
Phoebe buttons her lip, while the rest of the gang pretend they're not there.
Chandler's Office, Chandler is working as Nina knocks, then opens the door.

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: Do you have a sec?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ah, sure, Nina. What's up?

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: I don't know. For the past couple days, people have been avoiding me and giving me these really strange looks.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, well, ah... maybe that's because they're ah... jealous, of us.

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: Maybe. But that doesn't explain why they keep taking my scissors.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Ah, well, maybe that's, ah, because you're getting a big raise.

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: I am?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Sure, why not?

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: Oh my god! (Rushing over to give him a big hug) You're amazing!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh, you don't know. (Presses a button.) Helen, could you make sure we put through the paperwork on Miss Bookbinder's raise?

lines.speaker: Helen
lines.text: (Over the intercom) So you still want me to send her psychological profile to Personnel?

lines.speaker: Nina
lines.text: What?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Helen drinks. (Insincerely) Will you marry me?
Nina puts her hands on her hips, then gives Chandler a quizzical look.
Monica and Rachel's, Ross, Rachel, Chandler, and Phoebe are sharing a bowl of popcorn, while Monica carefully reads the instruction manual for her television set.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I ended up telling her everything.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, how'd she take it?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Pretty well. Except for the stapler thing. (He holds up a bandaged hand.) Little tip: if you're ever in a similar situation, never ever leave your hand... (he mimes Nina taking her revenge) ..on the desk.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay, I think I get how to do this.
Monica points the remote at her TV, and punches out a key combination from the book, but the dreaded SAP logo remains and Spanish still comes forth.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Alright, so, can we turn this off? Can we just make it... make them go away? Because I can't, I can't watch.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (Remotely turning off the television) okay, Pheebs, they're gone.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Are you alright?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. It's just, you know, it's this whole stupid Ursula thing, it's...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay, Pheebs, can I ask? So, he's going out with her. I mean, is it really so terrible?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um, yeah. Look, I mean, I'm not saying she's like evil or anything. She just, you know, she's always breaking my stuff. When I was eight, and I wouldn't let her have my Judy Jetson thermos, so she threw it under the bus. And then, oh, and then there was Randy Brown, who was like... Have you ever had a boyfriend who was like your best friend?

lines.speaker: Monica and Rachel
lines.text: (Wistfully, shaking their heads) No.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, but that's what he was for me. And she you know, kind of stole him away, and then... broke his heart... and then he wouldn't even talk to me any more. Because he said he didn't wanna be around... anything that looked like either one of us.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh... Oh, Pheebs.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I mean, I know Joey is not my boyfriend, or my thermos, or anything, but...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: You're not gonna lose him.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hon, you gotta talk to Joey.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah. Okay.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, come on, he doesn't know this stuff. If he knew how you felt.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: But he's falling in love with her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh please, they've been going out a week. They haven't even slept together yet, I mean, that's not serious.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay... Okay.
Monica and Ross indicate that they mean right now.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh, okay, oh.

Phoebe gets up and walks across the hallway, but the door to Chandler and Joey's apartment is shut. She knocks, and anxiously waits for Joey to come, but instead her identical twin sister emerges wearing one of Joey's shirts.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: (Surprised) Oh.
Phoebe reels back in shock, while Ursula defiantly leans against the doorpost as though she owns the place.

lines.speaker: Ursula
lines.text: Yeah, um, may we help you?
Closing Credits
Monica and Rachel's Balcony, Rachel is taking down the Christmas lights. Monica sees her, so she leans out of the small side window.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Rachel, what are you doing? It's freezing out here. Would you come back inside?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No no no no no. You wanted me to take them down, so... (she climbs onto the railing to reach the top of a pole) ..I'm takin' 'em down. Okay? Whoa! (Screams.)
Rachel slips, loses her balance, and falls over the edge..

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh-my-god Rachel! (Rushing out to look over the edge) Rachel!
In the apartment below, Mr.Heckles is trying to relax and read his newspaper, but Rachel is helplessly dangling upside-down with her ankle wrapped up in the Christmas lights.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (To Monica) I'm okay! I'm okay! (She knocks on Mr. Heckles's window.) Mr. Heckles, Mr. Heckles could you help me please?

lines.speaker: Mr.Heckles
lines.text: See, this is just the kind of thing I was talking about.
End
